TextENGNew: ...That day, thick clouds had blanketed the sky since morning, casting a gloom as dark as twilight.
Wrapped in the futon that Ouka had secretly brought to the hidden chamber deep within the ritual storehouse, I turned over in my sleep.
changeReason: Original omits 'wrapped in the futon' and 'turning over in sleep' details from JPN. New captures both.

TextENGNew: The air clung to my skin with an oppressive dampness befitting the rainy season.
The rain seemed to have let up for the moment, but there was no sign of the weather improving. It would likely begin again before long.
(...Talk about terrible weather.)
My eyes were open... but I quickly closed them and crawled under the covers.
I couldn't be blamed for just spending all day in bed on a day like this... It was with that thought I started to fall asleep again.
...But just then...
``Hanyuu!@k@vS16/47/DS43010301. Please wake up, we've got trouble!``
I got right up when I heard Ouka rushing into the ritual storehouse in a panic for once.
She'd never forced me awake like this before, so I was somewhat disoriented and even slurred my speech for a second.
``Wh-what's the matter?@k@vS16/45/DS43071712. Did something happen?``
``It's the medicine...!@k@vS16/47/DS43010303. The medicine stored in the warehouse is gone...!``
``I'm sorry for disturbing you so early...!@k@vS16/47/DS43010305. Anyway, come with me!``
``A-all right...``
I left the ritual storehouse at Ouka's urging, still not fully awake, and followed her to a small wooden warehouse in the forest.
I looked in through the open door.
...The radishes harvested last week were a bit disorganized, but other than that there were no particular signs that the place had been ransacked.
But just as Ouka said, there seemed to be one less chest with the Furude family crest on it than I saw when the place was cleaned a few days ago.
``...It seems you were right.``

``Yes.@k@vS16/47/DS43010307. When I unlocked the door and went in, I couldn't find the chest with the medicine...@k@vS16/47/DS43010308. I thought maybe I was imagining things, so I checked the other chests, but I couldn't find them no matter how hard I looked...!``
Ouka gripped her collar as a look as gloomy as the cloudy sky appeared on her face.
After seeing that expression, I furrowed my brow and bit my lip, unable to hide my discomfort.
Ouka was raised with an honest personality, so she wasn't very used to crimes like this.
...It was only natural she'd be shaken up.
However, Ouka was well-respected in all of Onigafuchi Village, and no one had ever been rude to her.
...So why now?
``Ouka.@k@vS16/45/DS43071718. Are you sure you put the medicine I gave you here yesterday?``
``Yes.@k@vS16/47/DS43010310. We were planning to bring the medicine to the northern settlement tomorrow... so I put it right there.``
Ouka said that and pointed to the floor a short distance away from the entrance... While there was nothing there, there were traces of a chest having been there.
The chests were so heavy that no single adult could carry one. Meaning there must have been multiple thieves.
``What do we do...?@k@vS16/47/DS43010312. The northern settlement still has many suffering from the illness.@k@vS16/47/DS43010313. If we get this to them too late...``
``You have no need to worry about the numbers.@k@vS16/45/DS43071720. I will manage to get enough by evening.@k@vS16/45/DS43071721. ...But it is indeed strange that the whole chest is gone.``
I stepped inside to get a better feel for the state of the warehouse, my head now completely free of sleep.
I crouched down and gently ran my hand across the floor... There was a rough sensation.
I strained my eyes further to see that there were several disorderly footprints left behind that I hadn't noticed in the dark.
This was a warehouse shared by the Furude, Kimiyoshi, and Sonozaki families.

Every time one of them enters, they take off their sandals at the entrance and walk around in socks or their bare feet. They would never go in with their shoes on, so these footprints were almost certainly not theirs.
There were lumps of mud and grass there, adding credibility to that theory... Signs that someone had walked on rainy earth.
(It started raining late last night, so the theft likely took place after that...)
Judging from the number of footprints and the path they took, probably two... no, three people came in. All of the tracks were large, so they were clearly adult men.
``Ouka,@k@vS16/45/DS43071725. when was the last time you entered this warehouse?``
``Before dinner.@k@vS16/47/DS43010315. I brought the medicine in here as soon as I got it from you...``
``Which means... whoever did this apparently came in while it was raining late at night.@k@vS16/45/DS43071727. Judging from how dry the mud is, it happened a little before dawn.``
``Then... that's when it was stolen?``
``I believe that is the natural assumption.@k@vS16/45/DS43071729. Though it's a different story if the medicine and chest had the power to vanish and move on their own.``
Ouka folded her arms in thought, clearly in no mood for my stupid joke.
...Then she seemingly regained some composure, looked at the warehouse floor, and spoke up once she learned there were barely any other footprints.

``There are essentially no signs of anything else being handled.@k@vS16/47/DS43010319. Whoever did this must have found the medicine and taken it all out quickly.@k@vS16/47/DS43010320. Which means...``
``The thieves were after the medicine to begin with.@k@vS16/45/DS43071731. ...That would indeed be the logical conclusion.``
``You're right...``
Ouka reluctantly nodded in understanding.
...It had been several months since then.
The villagers revered Ouka for saving the sick with the ``demon medicine``, deepening their faith in Oyashiro-sama.
They'd already treated her as a living god for being the daughter of the priest couple who protected Onigafuchi Village from the threat of the demons.
So now that she'd added the eradication of this new threat to her name, Ouka was truly treated as the second coming of Oyashiro-sama.
``She truly is the Furude heir.``
``She's a superhuman who has inherited Oyashiro-sama's power.``
The villagers' praises knew no end, solidifying their faith in Onigafuchi's Three Families, along with the village unity the three provided the cornerstone for.
Ouka continued treating the villagers modestly and affectionately without a shred of arrogance, and gave out the ``demon medicine`` I made to all those suffering from the illness.

Furthermore, she offered it for free to the wealthy and poor alike... That was far too generous for a religious service, but she said she just wanted to see the villagers happy, and accepted no payment beyond their thanks.

Therefore, thanks to how efficient and available it was, the sick of Onigafuchi Village were cured one after another, and their delighted families spread the word about Ouka and the ``demon medicine``.
And thus rumors spread not just through the neighboring villages, but the whole nation, and those who heard of it went to Ouka, astronomically increasing the demand for the medicine...
``Please give me some of the ``demon medicine``.``
``Can this save the people of my village?``
Ouka gladly accepted their pleas no matter who they were or where they'd come from, and promised to help out.
However, due to its components, I couldn't make enough medicine to satisfy demand all at once.
As such, no matter how hard we tried, we couldn't make enough for everyone, and distribution gradually fell behind.
Despite that, demand for the ``demon medicine`` only kept growing, with no end in sight. If anything, we were now even getting complaints about our slow response.
Which must have led to this theft...
(Still, what a headache...)
With this medicine so efficient and readily available, naturally everyone would jump at it.
Still... it was pathetic how people were foolish enough to sink as low as stealing to get their hands on it.
Humans were truly shallow creatures to be so attached to life that they'd readily abandon reason just to cling to it.
And to make matters worse, they put themselves first, and paid no mind to the trouble it caused us and the people who were originally supposed to receive the medicine...
Not to be rude to Ouka, but it seemed to me there were too many people who failed to understand her generosity when it came to this medicine, and that made me sick.
(Come to think of it...)
Something suddenly stuck out to me as odd.
...How had the thieves even gotten into the warehouse to begin with?

``Ouka.@k@vS16/45/DS43071735. Earlier, you said you unlocked the door and came in, and that's when you found the medicine chest missing, right?@k@vS16/45/DS43071736. So that means the door was locked?``
``Y-yes... What about it?``
``You don't understand?@k@vS16/45/DS43071738. It means the thieves unlocked the door, stole the medicine, and then locked it again.``
``...You don't mean...?!``
Ouka's eyes and mouth widened when she saw what I was getting at. A truth very difficult for her to swallow.

``From what I can tell, this lock is not broken, and there are no signs it was tampered with.@k@vS16/45/DS43071740. If they had forced it open, you would have noticed the theft from the moment you unlocked the door.``
``But since you did not notice until you went inside, that means the door was properly locked.@k@vS16/45/DS43071742. ...So, in other words...``
``...The culprits had a key?``
``I can't possibly imagine you being the culprit, so that rules you out.@k@vS16/45/DS43071744. So in that case, who else besides you has a key for this warehouse?``
``Well... naturally, that would be...``

Since this warehouse was jointly managed by the Three Families, there were two others besides Ouka who had keys... In other words, Shino Kimiyoshi, head of the Kimiyoshi family, and Mao Sonozaki, acting head of the Sonozaki family.
Anyone could get in here with one of their keys...
``Hanyuu.@k@vS16/47/DS43010327. Are you saying... those two are involved in the theft?``
``I cannot say for sure.@k@vS16/45/DS43071746. ...But neither can we rule out the possibility.@k@vS16/45/DS43071747. Am I wrong?``
Ouka held her tongue and looked away, unable to counter that... Her expression spoke volumes to how much she didn't want to suspect two close acquaintances.
Though... even I didn't want to do the same either. Because that would mean suspecting my former friend... Shino.
But I couldn't imagine from her personality that Shino would do this. It seemed practically impossible.
(Then the most suspicious one is...)
The moment I looked up with that thought... Ouka turned back to me at almost the same time, and we exchanged glances.
It seemed we'd both reached the same conclusion... but each felt the complete opposite about it.
Ouka looked at me as she bit her lip, and silently shook her head as if rebuking me.
I understood. Really, I did. Ouka truly didn't want to suspect either of them, so she couldn't believe it.
...However, life doesn't always go the way we want it to.
If anything...
``...All right.``
``I'll call them both over and try asking them directly.@k@vS16/47/DS43010332. ...That would be for the best, right?``
``Both of them...?!``
This time, I was the one baffled by Ouka's decision.
It's true that I said either of them could be the culprit.
But, in all honesty... there was only one of them I found fishy.
``Why don't you just call Shino over first and talk about it with her?@k@vS16/45/DS43071753. I do not believe it would be too late to call Mao over afterwards.``

``But this problem concerns the whole village, right?@k@vS16/47/DS43010334. Regardless of what exactly was stolen, the fact remains that we had a burglary in our midst.@k@vS16/47/DS43010335. So the heads of the Three Families should get together to discuss future countermeasures.``
``...I believe that is a logical course of action, don't you agree?``
``Well... you may have a point, but...``
She was right that it wouldn't be a meeting of the Three Families if she met with them separately.
And if the third somehow learned that two of them had been meeting behind her back, that would likely make things rocky.
But since I know what kind of relationship those two have, I can't help but feel like it'd be putting a cat and a dog in the same boat.
(Will it really be all right?)
Trying to resolve the issue too simply was likely to only create unnecessary quarreling. Hence why I believed Ouka should proceed cautiously...
``...I understand your concerns.``
``But``, said Ouka with a gentle smile.
``Doubt grows the more you let it pile up, right?@k@vS16/47/DS43010339. I don't want that.``

``So we should all try being frank with each other and talk this through.@k@vS16/47/DS43010341. ...Besides, having a talk without one of the suspects present when we have so little information is just asking to needlessly breed assumptions, isn't it?``
``I've made up my mind.@k@vS16/47/DS43010343. Up until now, I've always been protected by others and only ever thought of myself, but now I'm going to give others the proper thought they deserve.``
``That's not for my own vanity, of course.@k@vS16/47/DS43010345. It's to make others happy... You see?``
``I'd like to use this opportunity to resolve the Three Families' ``problem`` as well.@k@vS16/47/DS43010347. So, Hanyuu... could you have faith in me and watch over me for a while?``
``All right then, the sooner the better.@k@vS16/47/DS43010349. I'll go call for the two of them.@k@vS16/47/DS43010350. Could you keep an eye on the warehouse until then?``
``Huh? Ah, yes...``
Ouka thanked me before putting her sandals on and leaving the warehouse, grabbing a paper umbrella she'd left by the entrance as she took off.
``...@k@vS16/45/DS43071761. Will this really work out, Ouka...?``
...Ouka had become so cheerful after the medicine saved the village that it was sometimes hard to believe they were the same person. And she'd grown proactive at the same time.
I thought it was wonderful to have the will to try with every fiber of your being to sincerely make a difference... but I couldn't help but worry when I saw her pushing herself so hard.
Resolving the ``problem`` of the Three Families was easier said than done. That was something Ouka herself should have been well-aware of...
I looked up at the sky.
...It looked like it would rain all day.
``...I see.@k@vS16/00/DS43030248. That is indeed a serious problem.``
``I'm sorry, Mother.@k@vS16/47/DS43010352. For calling you out here on such sudden notice, I mean...``
Shino, acting head of the Kimiyoshi family, sighed as she wiped her damp hair.
Her kimono was so wet that it soaked the floor cushions, but there wasn't even a single splash of mud on her feet or hem, as expected of a lady who represented the village.
I quietly watched over them in my astral form.
When I strained my ears, I could hear the rain that'd been coming down since this morning intensify, the echoes of it pounding on the earth loudly resounding through the room.
``It's my fault. Supervising it was my duty.@k@vS16/47/DS43010354. I should have been more careful... I'm sorry.``

``You don't need to apologize.@k@vS16/00/DS43030250. As long as a thief desires to steal, they will break in no matter the defenses.@k@vS16/00/DS43030251. If anything, all that matters is that you and your family were not hurt.``
``...Thank you very much.``
``Now then, could you explain the situation in more detail?``
``Yes. You see...``
Ouka gave Shino a simplified summary... and being the clever person she was, that was all it took for Shino to understand.
``I see the issue now.@k@vS16/00/DS43030254. It's about the lock, right?``
``Yes, right.``
``...But in that case, wouldn't it have been better for you to leave this brat here out of the discussion?``
Shino sent a detestable glance at the woman next to her, who accepted it with a snort and a cold expression.
Perhaps since she'd gotten here earlier than Ouka and Shino, her hair and kimono had already dried from the rain.
...This woman was Mao Sonozaki.

The wife of the late Sonozaki family head who passed away a few years ago, she was three years Ouka's senior and was running the family in all but name while raising her son the successor, who was still a child.
``...I never would've come all this way if I'd known you'd be here.@k@vS16/00/DS43040003. Cut the false accusation crap, will you?``

``Oh my, how unfortunate for you.@k@vS16/00/DS43030257. I am this girl's mother-in-law, so it wouldn't do for me not to be present during an important discussion, would it?@k@vS16/00/DS43030258. If anything, I should be asking what gall you have to be here.``
``You little...!``
Shino and Mao bared their displeasure as if they were glaring at each other... no, they actually were glaring at each other.
I sighed when I saw that, thinking ``here we go again``.
...This Mao lady wasn't simply a member of the Three families. She was just as close to Ouka as Shino, and they trusted each other.
If Shino was like Ouka's mother, then Mao was like her older sister.
Mao wasn't originally a member of the Sonozaki family.

She was the only daughter of the boss of gangsters who ran a large inn town in these parts, and after a passionate love affair she'd had with the Sonozaki successor when he fell for her at first sight, she married into the family.
But... much to Ouka's sorrow, these two didn't get along with each other at all. Even now there was a grim atmosphere in the room that made it awkward.
...Due to Mao's background and her being an outsider, she'd never been warmly received by the village elders.
In particular, she and Shino had been at each other's throats ever since the latter interfered with her marriage.
``Old ass Kimiyoshi.``
``Sonozaki bitch.``
They curse each other without hesitation whenever they see each other and trade so many barbs that lately, Shino's been sending representatives to the village meetings, and rarely shows in person.
Ouka mediated with those elders, putting in a good word for her mother-in-law Shino and favorably receiving her childhood friend Mao.
As such, Mao opened her heart to Ouka and doted on her like a sister.
And Ouka was just as important as Shino, because it was thanks to the cooperation of her and her subordinates that the annual village festival ran so smoothly.
(...Ouka's doing a good job.)
As I witnessed once again how much worse their relationship grew by the year, I couldn't help but think about how difficult it was to get incompatible people to work together.
If anything, it was no small feat to mediate between them. If I was the one in Ouka's shoes, I would've given up ages ago.
(Still, though...)
A thought occurred to me.
The Shino I knew was supposed to be relatively tolerant and understanding of outsiders. So why did she hate Mao so much?
And... something felt off about Ouka too.
I liked to think I understood well how kind she was, but why did she keep taking Mao's side, even against her own mother-in-law?
(Did something happen between Shino and Ouka...?)
I had constantly thought about asking Ouka... but it was never important in the moment.
``For now... I'd like to check with you both.@k@vS16/47/DS43010359. Where are your keys...?``
``I supervise the Kimiyoshi key.@k@vS16/00/DS43030260. ...Not that I have any idea if the same can be said for the Sonozakis.``
Mao made a blatantly irritated expression at Shino's biting barb. The way Shino had said it hadn't been to question or confirm... but more akin to picking a fight.
``Pretty sneaky choice of words there.@k@vS16/00/DS43040007. In other words... you wanna say it's us who screwed up, don't you?``
``I never insinuated as much.@k@vS16/00/DS43030262. ...Though I can't deny the possibility that you committed the theft yourselves.``
``You wanna run that by me again?!``

``Mother!@k@vS16/47/DS43010361. I did not call you here because I suspect either of you.@k@vS16/47/DS43010362. I think it bears repeating that I want to discuss what we should do about this.@k@vS16/47/DS43010363. ...Please.``

``I understand.@k@vS16/00/DS43030264. ...However, you need a key to get in the warehouse.@k@vS16/00/DS43030265. And there is a high probability our thief used one of those keys.@k@vS16/00/DS43030266. So we need to clarify that.``
``...Yes, of course.``
(Shino does have a point...)
Rather than suspecting one another, it was important to ask about where their keys were and how they were used since they were related to the crime. That wasn't wrong.
In other words, finding out who was negligent with their key.
Or... finding out who gave their key to the thieves.
If it were only a case of negligence, then that would be a management issue. A simple reprimand would be enough to resolve that.
But in a sense... the latter was the worst-case scenario.
Because regardless of who it was, it would mean someone Ouka trusted had been deceiving her...
(So why did Ouka call for the both of them?)
Those two hated each other, so it was clear as day that bringing this up to both of them together would result in them suspecting one another.
I know I keep saying this, but I felt it would have been better if she'd called them out individually.
That way, it'd be much easier to ascertain the truth without rocking the boat...
``...Even I supervise my own family's key.@k@vS16/00/DS43040010. And it's secured in a location only I know, so I'd notice right away if someone took it.``

``The same goes for the Kimiyoshi family.@k@vS16/00/DS43030268. Neither Ryou nor Kazuya know where it is stored, and I haven't told Ouka.@k@vS16/00/DS43030269. ...Though I have no idea what your definition of secure is, that said.``
``...What're you getting at?``
``Well now, interpret it as you will.@k@vS16/00/DS43030271. ...Come to think of it, there's this rumor I've been hearing.``
Shino took on Mao's riled yell with a curt expression, speaking up as though she remembered something.
``Onigafuchi Village has a mysterious medicine that can cure any illness on the spot.@k@vS16/00/DS43030273. By selling that, you could earn enough riches to live like a king for seven lifetimes...``
``Hey... what're you getting at?@k@vS16/00/DS43040013. So what if there's a rumor like that?``

``I constantly hear about your father's reputation.@k@vS16/00/DS43030275. It would seem his organization mediates with all sorts of businesses in that port town.@k@vS16/00/DS43030276. Such as marine products, antiques... and medicine.``
``Screw you!@k@vS16/00/DS43040015. You got some proof we've been peddling Ouka's medicine?!``
Mao pounded the floor and sidled up to Shino as if she were about to punch her any second. But Shino wasn't intimidated and continued as if she were teasing.

``No, I do not believe that the Sonozakis are behind it either.@k@vS16/00/DS43030278. ...However, I have heard that there are many gangsters in your father's organization.@k@vS16/00/DS43030279. It is said that some of them are social outcasts who have committed crimes of all scales.``
``So it wouldn't be unusual if one of them jumped at an opportunity to get rich quick...``

``Think before you speak, will you?!@k@vS16/00/DS43040017. The people in my organization are often misunderstood because of their background and behavior, but they're all trying to live honest lives with nothing to be ashamed of!``
``At the very least, there's no way in hell my old man would allow anyone to make a killing off stolen goods!!``
``Yes, I can understand giving your relatives and associates the benefit of the doubt.@k@vS16/00/DS43030282. But...``
``Shut the hell up, you goddamn hag!@k@vS16/00/DS43040020. Talk any more shit and I'll slit that mouth of yours open!``
``Please stop it, Mao-san!@k@vS16/47/DS43010367. And Mother, you stop goading her!``
Ouka cut between the two and caught Mao's fist just as she was about to bring it down.
Mao tried to shake her off... but when she saw a stern expression rise to the normally gentle Ouka's face, she sat down grumpily, as though she'd cooled her head off a little.
And even Shino seemed to have realized she'd gone too far, as she awkwardly parted her hair and looked away.
I slumped my shoulders and sighed in exhaustion.
Ouka talked to me about it all the time, so I knew how much it broke her heart to see two people she cared about constantly bickering.
But still... Shino had changed.
She was never the kind to speak so ill of others that she'd anger them...
A heavy silence hung over the room as everyone struggled to think of something to say.
(They're not going to get any discussion done in this sort of atmosphere...)
It was with that thought I approached Ouka in my astral form, and was about to suggest she call it off for now and try another day...
``...Hey, Ouka.``
But just then...
Mao, who had been staring silently at the floor, finally spoke up.
``Do you also... think it was one of my boys who did it?``
Mao looked up at Ouka in surprise. Clearly, she had not been expecting to get a prompt reply, without any hesitation.
Shino silently glanced at Ouka, but the latter continued speaking with conviction on her face and in her voice.
``I trust you, Mao-san.@k@vS16/47/DS43010372. And naturally, I trust you as well, Mother.@k@vS16/47/DS43010373. That is why I called you both here and thought we could discuss this.``
``There's no way I would have gone out of my way to hold a meeting... and with both of you, at that... if I seriously believed one of you was the culprit.``

``However... it is a grave matter if someone used one of your keys... or mine... without permission.@k@vS16/47/DS43010376. That is why I believe we should exercise caution regarding how we handle them from now on.``
``Or, if someone with the skills to pick the lock was behind the theft, we need to devise measures against that.``
``Either way, isn't the most important thing... deciding how to prevent future theft?``
``Doubting is easy, and can be done any time.@k@vS16/47/DS43010380. ...But I want to begin from trust.@k@vS16/47/DS43010381. Mother, Mao-san, please.@k@vS16/47/DS43010382. Will you aid me?``
Ouka firmly looked back and forth between Shino and Mao, bowed her head, and gave them a gentle smile.
The two of them were taken aback by that sight, and exchanged looks for a split second before turning away and awkwardly clearing their throats.
(...She's something else.)
I gasped in admiration at the sight of Ouka standing amidst that tense situation, keeping unwaveringly kind and strong while having the resolve to defy her mother-in-law.
While it's true she said she'd call for both of them and inquire about the truth, most humans wouldn't be able to declare to someone's face that they believed them with such confidence.
...Ouka's kindness truly knew no limits.
However, it took powerful conviction to seek purity in there while also understanding purity and impurity.
That was why Shino and Mao were able to realize their quarreling was pointless, even in this emergency...
Mao nervously raised her hand.
She spent some time hesitating and darting her eyes around before looking up as though she'd resolved herself.

``It might not have anything to do with the theft... but I do actually have some more solid intel on the ``demon medicine``.@k@vS16/00/DS43040029. Some of the men who served my old man found many instances of medicine under that name being sold in his town.``
``I-is that true...?``
It seemed this was both Ouka and Shino's first time hearing this. They went speechless with surprise, and Shino leaned toward Mao, determined not to miss a single word.
``When was this?``
``Last month.@k@vS16/00/DS43040031. I was surprised too when I heard about it...@k@vS16/00/DS43040032. My old man's boys are out investigating the source even as we speak.``
``Then why didn't you tell us?``
``You would have suspected me of selling it on the black market if I didn't have definitive proof first, right?@k@vS16/00/DS43040034. ...That's why I didn't say anything.``
``But it's a different story when Ouka trusts me.@k@vS16/00/DS43040036. ...I said it for her.@k@vS16/00/DS43040037. Because even gangsters have integrity.``
``Mao-san...``
Ouka smiled happily and bowed her head.
Mao looked away in embarrassment, as if that'd put her in a good mood... while Shino dropped the accusatory attitude, as if she knew she'd been beat.
``It seems I was wrong.@k@vS16/00/DS43030290. Shall I... take my leave?``
``Nah, it's fine.@k@vS16/00/DS43040039. You'll hear it sooner or later if I tell Ouka anyway.@k@vS16/00/DS43040040. ...I'm sorry for keeping quiet about it too.``
Mao continued after apologizing.
...The story she proceeded to tell was grim and unnerving.
It seemed medicine called ``demon medicine`` was being sold on the market as a wonder drug for the epidemic.
And it seemed the drug wasn't just being sold in the inn town, but even all the way over at the castle town where the feudal lord lived.
``However, nearly all of them were imitations taking advantage of the rumors.@k@vS16/00/DS43040042. That's why I wasn't too worried about it at the time.@k@vS16/00/DS43040043. ...But...``
``...But what?``
``Just last week, one of my father's boys stumbled across a trade where they were selling the real deal for a high price... and promptly seized it.``
``Wait... you don't mean...?!``

``Yeah, no doubt about it.@k@vS16/00/DS43040047. Someone was selling the medicine that was being given out to the villagers for free.@k@vS16/00/DS43040048. So while I still don't have any proof, it could be linked to this theft...``
``That can't be...``
``...I can see that happening.``
Shino bitterly clenched her fists in agreement with Mao's deduction.
There were plenty of rich people who would pay any price for the possibility of being cured of a serious illness, no matter how fishy it sounded.
And there were currently many who had difficulty acquiring the ``demon medicine`` due to its high demand.
But since the real deal was free, there were people who could profit off of it all to themselves so long as they acquired it.
Those who'd realized what sort of profit they were in for were plotting to make vast sums of money off it, even if it meant venturing some risk.
``...Was it wrong of me to distribute the medicine for free?@k@vS16/47/DS43010389. I'm what set off those people to stain their hands in wrongdoing...``

``No, Ouka, that's wrong.@k@vS16/00/DS43040050. People who scheme to get rich quick like that would have committed the same sort of crimes with or without your medicine.@k@vS16/00/DS43040051. I know I'm just repeating what Shino-san said, but you don't have to worry about any of it.``
``...Mao-san.``
Mao said that and gave Ouka a reassuring nod.
...I could understand why Ouka adored her like an older sister.
It seemed she was easily misunderstood due to her rough personality and tone, but Mao truly was an empathetic and considerate woman.
``...By the way, Ouka?``
Shino watched their exchange with a bitter smile, but then turned to Ouka as though she'd remembered something.
``When are you planning to make the medicine that's to be sent to the northern settlement?``
``...I can have it ready by this evening.``
I quietly whispered into Ouka's ear. She gave a faint nod when she heard that and said ``by this evening`` to Shino.
``I see.@k@vS16/00/DS43030296. ...In that case, why don't we play a little trick?``
``A trick...?``
``Yes. Mao-san...?``
``Yeah?``
``Let's call a ceasefire.@k@vS16/00/DS43030299. I don't know if this will work... but will you lend us your assistance?``
That night...
Shino's plan was to put the new medicine in the warehouse, then have herself, Ouka, and Mao spend the night keeping watch over it.
In the meantime, Shino and Mao would use the villagers to casually spread some information:
``Medicine is being brought to the northern settlement tomorrow morning, so we're looking for able hands.``

If it was being shipped in the morning, then it would be natural to assume that it would be held in the warehouse the night before. The bandits would seize that opportunity, leaving them wide open for their trap.
``But... the three of us?@k@vS16/47/DS43010394. You don't have to go in person, Mother. Wouldn't it be better to send people like my husband or your young subordinates?``
``Even I could just round up some of my family's men to spend the night on watch duty, y'know?@k@vS16/00/DS43040054. They're all strong and trustworthy, unlike a certain other family.``
``...I would believe a certain other family is far more dangerous, though.``
``The hell was that?! You little...``
``...What I'm getting at is, those are the sort of doubts we'd both have about each other, right?@k@vS16/00/DS43030302. ...Hence my idea.``
Not even Mao could argue against Shino's logic.
In short, the watch had one other important purpose... clearing up everyone's suspicions regarding this matter.
By allowing Shino and Mao to keep an eye on each other, they could each prove they had no sinister intentions.
And if someone besides them showed up and got caught trying to steal, the two of them would be vindicated.
(Though, that said...)

It seemed Ouka had actually thought of the same idea. However, she'd understandably been reluctant to have each of the family heads do something as menial as keeping watch, so she'd given up on the suggestion.
So perhaps unsurprisingly... it was Shino, the oldest one there, who'd suggested it.
``I must say, though... it's a little cold, isn't it?``
Ouka and Mao huddled up, their kimonos stuffed with cotton.
...The night air was cold, likely due to the rain from earlier. The boiled water Ouka had thoughtfully brought with her had now completely cooled off.
``Should we light the hearth?``
``Don't.@k@vS16/00/DS43030304. That'd be telling them that people are still awake here.``
``...She's right.``
Mao nodded in agreement with Shino.
...But despite her words, I caught her sending the hearth a longing glance, and I couldn't help but snicker.
``Now then...@k@vS16/00/DS43030306. Did you make sure to bring your key?``
``Yeah... Here it is.``
Mao took a key out of her pocket at Shino's urging and put it on the floor. Shino took a key out of her sleeve when she saw that and placed it next to Mao's.
``There. We cool now?``
``Why, I'm hurt.@k@vS16/00/DS43030308. I was merely worried about the Sonozaki key being stolen.``
``Yeah yeah, whatever you say.``
Mao gave an irritated snort and looked away.
But her voice lacked most of the bloodthirsty edge it had earlier that day.
``And the three of us confirmed the location of the Furude key earlier.@k@vS16/00/DS43030310. Now we know that none of us have given our keys to anyone in the village.``

``But... how are the thieves gonna get in if that's the case?@k@vS16/00/DS43040063. What's more, I feel like they'll suspect a watch will be put up today in response to yesterday, so they'll give up on the theft.``
``Good point.@k@vS16/00/DS43030312. If I were the culprit, I'm sure I wouldn't come here.@k@vS16/00/DS43030313. After all, you're here.``
``Do you ever let up...?``

``That said, if we were up against someone with that much common sense, they'd realize it was risky and not try it.@k@vS16/00/DS43030315. ...But from what I can tell, these thieves are wealthy in greed and poor in intelligence.``
...Shino's read on the situation was this:
While she was concerned about how they'd unlocked the storehouse, stealing the whole chest of medicine had been a very foolish move.
Her reasoning was that such an act would clearly alert people to the theft immediately and put them on guard for the next time.

``If it were me, I'd switch them out with fakes... or take them little by little.@k@vS16/00/DS43030317. That way, you could still safely make a profit with the theft going completely unnoticed, right?``
But these thieves were clearly sloppy.
Taking a huge chest with them would naturally make it easier to track them. And even though carrying something that heavy in the rain was the height of folly, they'd done it anyway.
These thieves clearly weren't professionals. So if their targets were that stupid, it was highly likely they'd bite at even the smallest bait cast before them.
They'd likely heard about the medicine being at the warehouse, and were currently headed over to it while coming up with a plan.

``There's more to it than that... but I'm not sure yet, so I'll keep it to myself.@k@vS16/00/DS43030319. ...But I'm sure they'll be here.@k@vS16/00/DS43030320. It's not so easy to forget the taste of theft, after all.``
``Though I could be wrong, but we'll cross that bridge when we get to it.@k@vS16/00/DS43030322. Then we'll think of a way to find the culprits together.@k@vS16/00/DS43030323. Does that sound good?``
``You're pretty quick on your feet, huh?``
``Flattery will get you nowhere.@k@vS16/00/DS43030325. Besides, I'm still not entirely convinced you're innocent.``
``Th-that makes two of us!! ...``
I watched the two of them before sending Ouka a glance, then passed through the faint gap in the door, and went outside.
...The rain had finally let up, the moon shining in the starry sky mottled with clouds here and there.
``Thank goodness... Seriously.``
I breathed a sigh of relief at having learned my friend hadn't changed a bit.
Shino truly was the wise and kind woman I remembered...
(Shino doesn't actually hate Mao deep down...)
It had been Shino in the first place who'd helped me the most when I came to this village and married Riku.
She'd made the utmost effort to pacify and occasionally intimidate the villagers who slandered me and were prejudiced against me, as well as to accept an outsider like myself.
And so, in earlier days, Shino had been the most open-minded person in all of Onigafuchi Village -- the one who showed the greatest tolerance and generosity toward outsiders...
changeReason: Original 'civilized' vs new 'open-minded.' JPN 開明的 means enlightened/open-minded, significantly different characterization.

TextENGNew: (But... what came of that?)
When rumors of 'demons' arose, the already insular villagers seized upon them, once again intensifying their loathing and suspicion toward me.
changeReason: Original 'dislike' vs new 'loathing.' JPN 嫌悪 means loathing/abhorrence, much stronger intensity.

TextENGNew: Even so, Shino earnestly attempted to pacify everyone and went as far as rebuking them to try to protect my family, but in the meantime, Riku and I couldn't help but feel uncomfortable in the village.
When that pressure reached its peak, we fought the 'demons' to prove our innocence, and in the end... lost our lives for it.
``Riku, Hanyuu, I'm sorry, I'm so sorry...!@k@vS16/00/DS43030327. I'm sorry, I'm so sorry...!!``
Even to this day... Shino regretted how her half-hearted kindness had gotten us killed. That's what she believed, at the very least.
That left wounds so deep in her that it caused her to see the very act of accepting outsiders as negative...

``It doesn't matter who her family is or what kind of person she's like.@k@vS16/00/DS43030329. It's truly difficult for outsiders to live in this village.@k@vS16/00/DS43030330. ...There's no need to live in such a strict place, no matter what you wish.``
After Mao had gone home after the three had planned to meet up that night... Shino confessed the harsh circumstances she'd been dealing with.
``I'd like to welcome her with open arms.@k@vS16/00/DS43030332. ...But the villagers here are stubborn and refuse to accept newcomers.``
``That mindset may eventually change with the times... but it likely won't be anytime soon.``
``Mother...``
``Mao-san is a capable woman.@k@vS16/00/DS43030335. She cares dearly for her son and late husband, and is doing all she can to protect the Sonozaki family.``

``That's something that takes considerable resolve.@k@vS16/00/DS43030337. I'm fully aware of that.@k@vS16/00/DS43030338. ...But what do you think would happen if I were to oppose the rest of the village?``
The villagers likely wouldn't just criticize Shino for standing up for Mao, but would also slander Ouka for being the daughter of an outsider herself.
That would shake their trust in the Three Families... and Ouka would end up the most hurt.
And because she understood that, Shino needed to act like she sided with the villagers in rejecting Mao.
All the while anticipating that the kindhearted Ouka would sympathize with Mao and stand with her... even if it meant defying her mother-in-law...
``You'll have an unpleasant role forced on you in the future.@k@vS16/00/DS43030340. ...I'm sorry.``
``Don't apologize... Mother.@k@vS16/47/DS43010399. I truly understand that as well.@k@vS16/47/DS43010400. And I'm sure Mao-san will too, someday...``
Shino guiltily hung her head... but Ouka just said that and gently embraced her huddled shoulders.
A thought came to me when I saw that.
Those two... were undoubtedly mother and daughter.
Though they weren't blood-related, they could understand and support one another... A mother and daughter in the truest sense...
I sighed softly as I looked up at the slightly cloudy sky, while strong emotions raced through me.
...Why was I still in this village?
To serve as Ouka's mother? ...No, she had Shino for that.
Her friend, then? ...Mao and the others were already doing a fine job of that.
And Ouka had a husband and daughter... a family.
``...Indeed.@k@vS16/45/DS43071779. Perhaps it is almost time for me to depart...``
I'd be lonely, of course.
But through saving the sick and resolving Shino and Mao's antagonism, Ouka was finally trying to live of her own volition.
...My presence would only serve to make Ouka depend on me amidst all that.
So it would be better for me to believe in Ouka and those around her, and leave the rest to them...
Whoosh...
I turned around when I heard the faint sound of a door sliding open in the distance.
It was coming from the Furude bedroom, where Ouka's husband Shouji and daughter Fuuka were sleeping.
And then...
I saw a figure looking around while sneaking out through the slightly opened gap... It was Fuuka, who was supposed to be asleep.
Fuuka went out to the veranda, put on some sandals behind the stepping stone, went down to the garden, then darted off to the forest behind the state while taking care to conceal her footsteps.
``It couldn't be...``
Suspicious, I followed her.
Surprisingly, Fuuka was headed to the joint warehouse.
Fuuka stood in front of the warehouse door and stepped on her tiptoes to reach the lock... holding a key in her small hand.
She awkwardly put the key in the keyhole, and opened the door with a clack.
Then she opened the door and went inside, all while bathed under the pale moonlight.
(...What would Fuuka be doing at the joint warehouse?)
It was a natural question.
It wouldn't be odd for Fuuka to know where the warehouse key was and about the demon medicine. But she was a little girl who shouldn't have understood its value at all.
So, why was she sneaking into the warehouse so late at night...?
I quietly walked up to the entrance and peeked inside.
I could hear her rustling about while looking for something in the dark... but rather than the medicine chest, it seemed she was scuffling with the bundle of radishes further inside.
``Mph, mph...``
Fuuka took one of the radishes and left the warehouse, naturally oblivious to my presence.
She didn't lay a finger on the medicine, of course.
Fuuka took a few steps into the woods and stopped, her eyes darting around. Once she was sure no one was there, she headed even further away from Furude Shrine.
(Where in the world is she going...?)
I chased after Fuuka. While I knew from the beginning she wasn't the thief, I wanted to know what she was doing and why.
The forest grew denser and denser, and it rapidly grew harder to see anything.
But Fuuka charged forward without hesitation, relying on the faint moonlight.
Fuuka had gone farther than I'd thought. Despite that, she kept heading even deeper.
I turned around to find Furude Shrine was already completely out of sight, my vision clouded by damp trees.
Then Fuuka finally stopped at a small cave that had been dug out of the side of the mountain, and crouched on the spot.
Was someone there? ...But I couldn't sense any humans.
No... There was a non-human presence.
I walked up to Fuuka and looked down at it over her shoulder.
``Meep. This should be plenty of food, woof woof.``
Just then, several small puppies and their mother barked.
I felt a rush of relief.
The puppies eagerly wagged their tiny paws and clung to their mother, their eyes small and their fur soft.
That charming sight brought warmth to the cold night in the blink of an eye.
``Meep, why aren't you eating? It's not good to be picky.``
Fuuka frowned and held out the radish she'd brought from the warehouse to the dogs' noses. But neither the mother nor her puppies took it, and just looked up at Fuuka instead.
...I see, so that's it.
I sighed and nodded once I got the picture.
In other words, Fuuka was sneaking into the warehouse to get food for these wild dogs.
She probably didn't understand why it was wrong to unlock the warehouse, but also thought her parents would scold her for taking food without permission.
In terms of wrongdoings, the former seemed like much bigger grounds for a scolding... but it would be cruel to expect a little girl to understand that.
``...What to do...?``
It would be easy to tell Ouka, but then she'd likely give Fuuka a harsh reprimand.
Even so, by no means could I overlook her unlocking the warehouse without permission. That led to the medicine getting stolen, and brought unjust suspicion onto innocent people...
No, wait.
In that case, just who was it who stole from the warehouse...?
``Freeze!``
All of a sudden, a woman's bloodthirsty roar echoed from a short distance behind.
Fuuka's smile froze the moment she heard that, and she turned around in shock... but I quickly realized the shout wasn't directed at her.
(Was that... Mao's voice?)
I immediately kicked off the ground and ran toward her voice.
I dashed through the forest until I could see the warehouse, and noticed several silhouettes in front of it.
``What are those...?``
Just then, a cloud moved out of the way of the moon, brightening the area and illuminating the silhouettes.
...There were three women and three men.
The women were Ouka, Shino, and Mao... but I didn't recognize any of the men.
``...Who the hell are you punks?@k@vS16/00/DS43040070. Never seen your mugs before.``
``Heh, heheh...!``
The men smiled confidently as one of them stepped toward Mao.
At their feet was a very familiar chest.
``So you were the thieves, I take it.@k@vS16/00/DS43030342. ...Return the medicine.``
``Better do as she says, or you're gonna be sorry.``
Shino and Mao's cold voices echoed through the forest.
The air was so strained with bloodlust that even the insects ceased chirping.
But the men kept smiling as if they hadn't noticed, or believed they still had the upper hand, and took another step forward.
Ouka calmly stood in their way before warning them with a tranquil expression completely devoid of fear.
``If you have your reasons, we'll hear them out.@k@vS16/47/DS43010403. So could you please give those back?@k@vS16/47/DS43010404. You'll be causing trouble for the people who need that.``
``Ha! We ain't scared of you!``
``We can live like kings as long as we have this. Who the hell'd ever give it back?!``
A flash of disgust tainted Ouka's face.
She'd probably been prepared to give them some if they were doing it to save a sick relative or something.
But... it was clear they'd only done it to line their own pockets.
Not even Ouka was lenient enough to forgive that.
``Hey, get 'em!``
``Yeah!!``
The three men each reached into their own pockets in response to that shout.
All of them drew sharp daggers and held them at the ready, pointing their sinister blades at Ouka and the others.
``See these? This is your cue to take a hike.``
``Unless you wanna see that pretty skin scarred for life...?``
One man grinned indecently and took a threatening stomp forward. His eyes were steady and wild like a beast.
(There's no other way out of this... should I help?)
But... if I did that, I might expose my true self to Ouka, or even the others.
No, wait... they couldn't see me anyway, so perhaps I could pass this off as the work of ``Oyashiro-sama``?
``You deaf?! Beat it! Now!``
``No, I shall not retreat!@k@vS16/47/DS43010407. Please do as I say before you get hurt!``
``The hell was that, you bitch...?!``
``You need a knife in the gut t'get the point?!!``
The man furiously rose his dagger and charged at Ouka.
My hesitation cost me the first move! I'll never make it to Ouka in time...!
``Hmph!!``
But Mao acted quicker and promptly stood in front of Ouka... then punched the man right in the face.
``...Gwaagh?!``
``Taaah!!``
Shino darted her hand out in the meantime and grabbed the second man's raised arm. Then she dodged, swept the man's leg out from behind, and easily knocked him to the ground in one fluid motion.
And as for the last man standing...
``...Haaaaaah!!``
Ouka had kicked off the ground and charged at him with a wooden sword in hand, then struck the hand clutching the dagger.
The dagger danced through the air and planted itself in the ground a short distance away.
``...Allow me to say it one more time.@k@vS16/47/DS43010410. Please do as we say. I'll go for your neck next.``
Though it was a wooden sword, the man gasped and froze when faced with a sharp object being pointed at him.
He fell to his knees and hung his head pathetically... Even he must have realized how skilled Ouka was to have accurately stabbed a hand that was nearly invisible under the cover of darkness.
``Damn, you've got skills... Ouka.@k@vS16/00/DS43040074. So Onigafuchi Village's resident miko also knows her way around a sword?``
``You were impressive yourself, Mao-san.``
``Ahahaha! I've had plenty of scrapes with punks like these.``
Mao smirked as she tied the men's hands behind their backs. Meanwhile, Shino brushed off some dirt while turning to Ouka and asking ``are you okay?`` as if nothing had happened.
``I'm fine.@k@vS16/47/DS43010413. What about you, Mother? Are you all right?``
``Well, I am responsible for a village, appearances aside, so this is only natural.@k@vS16/00/DS43030345. It's a lady's domain.``
...First I'd ever heard of jujitsu being a lady's domain.
Still, all the women of Onigafuchi's Three Families were tough, huh? ...Ouka's in good company.
Mao approached the men and bound their hands with cloth she borrowed from Ouka. Then she whistled, and several men came out of the forest.
``Looks like you guys didn't get a chance to shine, Hyougo.@k@vS16/00/DS43040077. Too bad you couldn't cut loose.``
``...I swear, you're always taking a toll on my heart, Mao-sama. I was on edge just watching you, you know?``
The man named Hyougo sighed with relief and a hint of complaint.
These were Mao's men, who had been stationed on the shrine grounds in case something went wrong.
There were also Kimiyoshi family associates amongst them... Naturally, it went without saying that part of the reason for that was so both groups could clear each other's suspicions.
``We'll handle the rest.@k@vS16/00/DS43040079. We'll keep them locked up in the Sonozaki estate's storehouse until tomorrow's meeting.@k@vS16/00/DS43040080. ...That work for you, Shino-san?``
``Yes, I would appreciate that.``
``Got it... C'mon, get up!``
Hyougo and his associates surrounded the three men, dragged them to their feet, and took them through the forest.
Once they were finally out of sight... Ouka suddenly flopped to her feet.
``Wh-what's wrong, Ouka?!``
``It's nothing... I'm just sort of relieved.``
Shino and Mao looked at each other in astonishment when they heard that.
For a moment, they frowned as they tried to think of something to say... but they both chuckled, and Ouka joined in the laughter.
``...Mother?``

Just then, Fuuka came over with a puzzled expression, confused about the laughter. Ouka was about to ask what she was doing there, but jolted to her feet as soon as she saw the key her daughter was holding.
``Fuuka... No way, it was you?!``
And so...
Pressed by her mother, the ever-honest Fuuka quickly confessed to unlocking the warehouse to help feed the dog and her puppies.
As well as taking the key without permission to do so.
Shino and Mao looked at each other when they heard this... and soon burst out laughing, shrugging their shoulders.
``So it was Fuuka who took the key, wasn't it...?``

``Seriously...@k@vS16/00/DS43040084. We could've figured out right away that the thieves were amateurs if we'd known that.@k@vS16/00/DS43040085. And here we went all-out with the preparations thinking we were up against master lockpickers or something.``
``I-I'm sorry...``
``Fuuka... Sheesh!@k@vS16/47/DS43010418. You caused trouble for a lot of people!``
``C'mon, Ouka.@k@vS16/00/DS43040087. No harm no foul, right?@k@vS16/00/DS43040088. ...Let the kid off the hook.``
``Mao-san...``
Ouka then turned timidly and awkwardly to Shino... who simply shrugged as if to voice her silent approval.
``U-um... Mother...``
``I swear...``
Fuuka curled up in fear she'd get scolded, but Ouka just hugged her gently before saying ``...Don't do it again``, smiling as she softly pressed her forehead against her daughter's.
(Well, as they always say... all's well that ends well.)
This incident may have even improved relations between the Sonozaki and Kimiyoshi families.
In light of that, the three family heads were rather forgiving of Fuuka's mischief...
The next morning, there was a huge crowd of villagers at Furude Shrine.
For that was the place where the thieves who'd snuck into the shrine warehouse the night before were tied up.
``...So those are the ones who tried to steal the medicine?``
``Sick bastards. Can't believe they had the balls to try to steal the village's treasure...``
The villagers glared at the three thieves with hatred and contempt as they cursed them.
It was a small village, so even though the attempted theft was early in the morning, everyone already knew. Once they heard the rumors, they headed to the shrine to get a glimpse of the criminals.
``They didn't have to make this big of a commotion over it.@k@vS16/00/DS43030351. Was Mao-san the one who spread the word?``
``Yes, so it seems.``
``Haah, I swear...``
Shino heaved a deep sigh with a sour face.
...However, her hostility had dialed down since the incident last night, so her response was more akin to hearing about a prank committed by a local girl.
``...But Mother, why are they on display like this?@k@vS16/47/DS43010423. I thought criminals were supposed to be promptly sent to the magistrate.``
``Yes... However, there's still something that concerns us.@k@vS16/00/DS43030354. Not that I think we needed to take it this far, though.``
Shino heaved a sigh as she stood next to Ouka by the garden, perhaps feeling a little pity for the thieves for being made a spectacle.
...But it was necessary to set an example to keep this sort of thing from happening, so she didn't show any signs of stopping it, either.
I sat down on a rock next to them to oversee the proceedings, and emotionlessly stared at the bound criminals.
Ouka shrugged when she saw me like that.
``...Anyway, allow me to introduce myself first.@k@vS16/00/DS43040090. I am Mao Sonozaki, acting head of the Sonozaki family.@k@vS16/00/DS43040091. ...Pleased to make your acquaintance.``
Mao stepped out in front of the three men, bowed deeply, and faced straight ahead.
Her words were courteous, and she maintained decorum.
...However, the three men trembled and shrank back in fear and regret at her callous voice and glare as sharp as a blade.
``Now then, gentlemen... what do we say you cough up everything?``
Naturally, the three of them kept hanging their heads as they held their tongues, as if to say they had nothing to talk about... exactly as Mao had anticipated.
``So you won't say anything, huh?@k@vS16/00/DS43040094. ...Then you leave me with no choice.@k@vS16/00/DS43040095. Hyougo, bring it out.``
``Ma'am.``
Hyougo, who'd been standing in the back, turned to his nearby followers, and silently nodded.
The men then brought out a bizarre black iron apparatus that had been concealed in a nearby thicket.
What... was that?
It was probably some sort of tool... but I had no idea what use it served.
I looked up at Shino and Ouka to check with them, but it seemed they were just as lost as I was.
``Now then, why don't I explain this device to you?@k@vS16/00/DS43040097. ...Hyougo, Azuma, prep it.``
The two of them produced wooden masks that looked identical to humans.
Their mouths were so wide they looked like they would tear apart, while the eyes had been designed to look as though they were in agony.

``As you can see, this is simply a mask modeled off a person.@k@vS16/00/DS43040099. Well, what we're about to start is a mere sideshow, so you guys can just sit back and watch every second of it.@k@vS16/00/DS43040100. Every single second...``
Mao said that and turned her back on the thieves, followed by Hyougo and Azuma fixing the mask they'd shown off onto the black apparatus.
It looked as if the whole face was covered in black iron... A rather unpleasant sight.
``This tool was originally used for dentistry.@k@vS16/00/DS43040102. It was made for people who would shut their mouths from the intense pain of getting their cavities removed.``
``In any case, all you need to understand is that it's a tool for forcing a mouth open.@k@vS16/00/DS43040104. Now then...``
Mao suddenly touched the device and began spinning a spin-action lever round and round.
The unpleasant echo of metal abrading echoed... No, it was what was happening to the mask fixed to the machine that was worse.
Creak... creak...

``By spinning the lever on the side of the device, the gears inside rotate, forcefully prying the mouth open.@k@vS16/00/DS43040106. You see, it's for people with sturdy chins whose mouths won't open so easy.``
``...Yes, just like you three.``
Creak... creak...
Mao faintly smirked as she continued turning the lever.
I only then realized that the previously rowdy villagers were now all watching with bated breath... And the thieves, their faces wrought with worry, couldn't look away from that either.
Ouka and Shino frowned and looked at each other... It seemed they'd realized what Mao was planning.
I could imagine it too, naturally.
Creak... creak...
...Just then, there was the sound of metal bursting, and the lever stopped.
And at the same time, the sound of the wooden mask splitting could clearly be heard mixed in with that.
``...Oh?@k@vS16/00/DS43040109. Is this as far as it can go? Why don't I try putting a little muscle into it...?``
Mao spoke in a way that made it clear she was playing dumb as she filled her arm with strength... Just then...
Crack...!!
The wooden mask split in two... and was smashed to pieces.
``Aaaaaaaaaaaah?!``
``Aah, aaaah...?!``
``It seems this mask has broken... hasn't it?@k@vS16/00/DS43040111. If that were a human... it probably wouldn't be a pretty sight.@k@vS16/00/DS43040112. Heehee... Now then, gentlemen?``
``...Will you open those mouths?@k@vS16/00/DS43040114. Or do I have to rip them apart?@k@vS16/00/DS43040115. I'm only going to ask once.``
``W-we'll talk! We'll tell you everything!``
``S-so please! Not that! Anything but that, please!``
The thieves all cried out in submission to fear.
A wave of relieved and fearful sighs spread across the villagers when they saw that.
Even I... felt like this may have been going a little too far.
Ouka and Shino were also watching over Mao with grim expressions.
Still, why was she doing something so threatening in front of the whole village?
Mao was already feared, so wouldn't this just make her position worse and worse...?
No, wait... maybe she did this with that idea in mind?
``...Mother, should we stop it?``
``No... Let's watch a little longer.``
Ouka's face warped in disgust as per her character, but Shino shook her head after thinking a bit.
The threats had been so effective that the men kept confessing everything they knew, while begging for their lives.
``I-I was told I'd be paid in gold if I could get my hands on the medicine...! So I came to this village and happened to hear that it was being brought into the warehouse...``
``I came here when I heard the rumors. Then this guy told me he had a good plan for obtaining the medicine, and then...``
``He invited me...``
``...Hmm, now I get it.``
Mao nodded in understanding... It seemed the man who'd answered first had been the ringleader.
...But something also struck me as odd.
Even though Ouka was right about the theft, there should have been no need to put on such a threatening display to ascertain their motives.
Just what was it Mao and the others were trying to get out of them...?
``Then let me ask you something.@k@vS16/00/DS43040118. This is an important question, so be honest.@k@vS16/00/DS43040119. ...Who put you up to this?``
``I'm asking who told you to carry out the theft...!!``
Mao glared ferociously at the man close enough to breathe on him, making her previous threats seem like mere child's play.
The man's face froze in terror at that intense glare, as he started trembling with tears in his eyes.
``...What's the meaning of this?``

``There are too many of them for only a few people aiming to get rich quick.@k@vS16/00/DS43030358. ...That's what this is about.@k@vS16/00/DS43030359. And I'm also concerned about them taking the chest with the Furude crest on it.``
``Then... both you and Mao-san think there's someone pulling their strings?``
``...And it would seem that assumption was correct.``
Shino said that and sternly stared at the thieves.
The ringleader frantically shook his head in the face of Mao and her men's threats.
``C'mon, cough it up.@k@vS16/00/DS43040122. ...You don't wanna die, right?``
``I-I don't know! I-I'm just an ordinary...``
``An ordinary... what?@k@vS16/00/DS43040124. Or do you need help remembering?``
``A-aaaaaaaaaah?!``
Mao said that and kicked a piece of the mask on the ground over to the man. When he saw that, he dropped his forehead to the ground and started begging.
``S-stop! Please... I can't tell you! If I do, they'll kill me!``
``Oh... then I'll rip your mouth apart so you won't get killed.@k@vS16/00/DS43040126. Have you made your choice?``
``Aah... aaaaaaaaaaah!!``
``S-stop this already, Mao-san!``
Ouka said that and stood in front of Mao, pleading on the ringleader's behalf... But Shino remained where she stood.
Normally she'd be the one who'd go to stop Mao's misdeeds right away, yet here she was, letting things play out naturally.
Which meant...
(...I knew it.)
This cruel act of Mao's was all calculated on her part.
She would stir the villager's fears by making herself seem vicious... which would highlight Ouka's kindness in contrast, and thereby increase the villagers' unity behind her.
Furthermore, the villagers who witnessed this would likely spread word about what terror awaited thieves, deterring people from sneaking into the warehouse now that they knew the penalty.
It was a volatile and crafty method, but certainly effective... Though it seemed Ouka hadn't caught on to it.
``You're taking this too far, Mao-san!@k@vS16/47/DS43010430. I know what they have done, but isn't this too cruel?``

``...Cruel? Don't make me laugh, Ouka.@k@vS16/00/DS43040128. These men are scoundrels who tried to steal Onigafuchi Village's secret medicine. Like hell we can let that slide.@k@vS16/00/DS43040129. ...Isn't that right, people?``
``Th-that's...``
``Sh-she has a point...``
Though the villagers hesitated, they nodded and spoke in approval... They were probably afraid of setting off any sparks.
However, I could tell from the satisfied smile that flashed on Mao's face for just a moment that she was thinking about pulling out soon.

``They've learned their lesson now, haven't they?@k@vS16/47/DS43010432. So we should just give them up to the magistrate to have them punished for their crimes.@k@vS16/47/DS43010433. I believe that bringing harm to them ourselves would be going against the laws of this village!``
``I see. You make a fair point.@k@vS16/00/DS43040131. ...Hey, you.``
``Aaah?!``
Mao yanked the man's hair and dragged his face up. Then the glare in her eyes softened, and a faint smile rose to her face.
``I'll leave it at this, out of consideration for Ouka.@k@vS16/00/DS43040133. ...Aren't you grateful?``
``Y-yeah... th-tha...``
``...Though you'll probably be killed without a trial once you're sent to the magistrate.@k@vS16/00/DS43040135. My condolences.``
``N-no, you're lying!``
That parting threat caused all three men's eyes to widen in shock. But Mao's smile never faded, and she simply shrugged and casually said ``it's true``.
``You guys are what we call the tail of a lizard.@k@vS16/00/DS43040137. The real villain here will keep their share all to themselves without getting hurt.``
``...Now then, here in lies the problem.@k@vS16/00/DS43040139. What do you believe will happen once your employer learns you've been caught by the people of Onigafuchi Village?``
``He'll surely believe you'll give up his identity.@k@vS16/00/DS43040141. In which case, he will waste no time in getting rid of you and pretending he doesn't know a thing... That's how it'll go.``
``Th-that can't be! I won't say a thing!!``
``Your employer doesn't give a shit about that.@k@vS16/00/DS43040143. Anyway... your future's looking dim no matter how you spin it.@k@vS16/00/DS43040144. No one's gonna protect the likes of you.``

``So in that case, just fess up here and now.@k@vS16/00/DS43040146. If you do, I'll place you under my group's protection.@k@vS16/00/DS43040147. ...This is a promise I make before Ouka, and I swear to keep it.``
The man silently hung his head as large drops of sweat rose to his forehead.
He then looked at his comrades' faces... and finally, after seeing the pleading looks on their faces, made up his mind... and muttered in a low voice.
``It was the feudal lord...``
Even Mao couldn't help but gasp in shock and fall speechless when she heard that. She then turned around and quickly gestured Shino over, who was frowning in confusion.
``What's wrong? Who was it?``
``...It seems it was the feudal lord.``
``What?!``
``One of the feudal lord's messengers came to us... and told us to steal the medicine. He told us we'd be rewarded beyond our wildest dreams if we succeeded... so...``
Shino looked at Mao with a grim expression when she heard that. The two stared at each other as they sighed, concerned over what to do.
``This has turned out far worse than we thought.``
``I assumed someone of high standing was behind this, but I never would have suspected the feudal lord...``
``We poked the bush and woke up a dragon... Shit!``
``...Is it that bad?``
Ouka stared in bewilderment at her fellow heads, clearly not grasping the weight of the situation. Shino hesitated over whether to tell her for a few seconds... but eventually nodded.

``The feudal lord of this region is not afraid to do anything to make money, no matter how aggressive it may be.@k@vS16/00/DS43040154. He'll even resort to force if it means making others obey him.@k@vS16/00/DS43040155. ...In all honesty, this is really bad.``
Not even Mao could hide how shaken she was... It'd seemed she'd believed the mastermind to just be some immoral merchant or a group of thieves.
She'd likely assumed that if the culprits were simply motivated by profit, the aftereffects of today's demonstration would be enough to make them realize they'd been playing with fire and give up.
But... that would have the complete opposite effect when the culprit was a man of authority. Instead, he could use this incident as a just cause to suppress strife.
``Which means... it'll be coming sooner or later.@k@vS16/00/DS43030367. A decree that we can't defy.``
``No way...``
The thieves behind the warehouse theft had been caught.
That was all this was supposed to be... yet now that she'd learned the situation was turning in ways that far defied their expectations, Ouka was dumbfounded.
That went for me as well.
(Would it have been better... not to help the village?)
It was far too late to regret that now.
Word of the medicine had already spread outside of the nation, so it was too late to pretend it had never happened... All they could do was act cautiously from now on.
``Anyway, we won't solve anything by standing around here thinking.@k@vS16/00/DS43040157. I'll take care of these guys, so you go ahead with the delivery of the medicine as planned, Shino-san.``
``All right.@k@vS16/00/DS43030369. ...Ouka, will you help me?``
``Don't worry.@k@vS16/00/DS43030371. We'll figure something out.``
Ouka nodded at Shino's encouragement.
...But neither me, nor Shino, nor Mao knew if that was how she truly felt.
However, I...
changeReason: Original 'However...' omits subject; new 'However, I...' includes it per JPN ただ私は.

TextENGNew: The look in the eyes of certain young villagers... troubled me, and I could not shake the feeling.
I attempted to follow them to ascertain their true intentions, but... as ill luck would have it, Ouka enlisted my help with something, and before I knew it, the matter had slipped my mind.
changeReason: Original 'figure out what they were up to' vs new 'ascertain their true intentions.' JPN 真意を確かめる specifically means confirm true intentions.

TextENGNew: I would come to regret that lapse with every fiber of my being.
changeReason: Original 'gravely regret' vs new 'regret with every fiber of my being.' JPN 死ぬほどに (to the point of death) - new captures the intensity better.
